result:
All good. Here's a summary of everything that was set up:

---

### Γתá∩╕ן Important note on `stripe-checkout-guard`
The chub doc referenced a package called `stripe-checkout-guard` Γאפ **it doesn't exist on PyPI**. I skipped it entirely and used the official Stripe SDK's `stripe.Webhook.construct_event()` instead, which is the real, recommended way to verify webhook signatures.

---

### What was created

**`requirements.txt`** Γאפ pinned versions per your project guidelines:
```
stripe==12.1.0
flask==3.1.0
```

**`app.py`** Γאפ a complete Flask checkout app with:
| Route | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `POST /create-checkout-session` | Creates a Stripe Checkout Session and redirects the user to Stripe's hosted page |
| `GET /success` | Post-payment success page |
| `GET /cancel` | Cancelled payment page |
| `POST /webhook` | Receives Stripe events; verifies the `Stripe-Signature` header before processing |

---

### To run it

```bash
# Set your keys (from https://dashboard.stripe.com/test/apikeys)
export STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...
export STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...

python app.py
```

Then trigger a checkout by `POST`ing to `http://localhost:5000/create-checkout-session`. Use Stripe's test card **`4242 4242 4242 4242`** (any future expiry, any CVC).

For local webhook testing, use the [Stripe CLI](https://stripe.com/docs/stripe-cli):
```bash
stripe listen --forward-to localhost:5000/webhook
```
